title: Register investigator in core agent and config registries
description: Add `investigator` to the core agent-type registries and config defaults while keeping it out of mutating-agent sets. Update repo and bundled config defaults so runtime validation and config parity stay aligned.
acceptance_criteria: `src/agent_takt/models.py` includes `investigator` in `AGENT_TYPES` and excludes it from `MUTATING_AGENTS`.
Default config surfaces include `investigator` consistently in code, bundled defaults, and repo config.
Config tests reflect the new agent type and continue to assert repo/default parity.

handoff_summary.completed:
- `models.py`: Added `"investigator"` to `AGENT_TYPES`; `MUTATING_AGENTS` unchanged.
- `config.py`: Added `"investigator"` to `OrchestratorConfig.agent_types` field default and in `default_config()` function.
- `_data/default_config.yaml`: Added `investigator` to `agent_types` list.
- `.takt/config.yaml`: Added `investigator` to `agent_types` list (keeps repo config in parity with defaults, as verified by `test_repo_config_matches_defaults`).
- `tests/test_config.py`: Updated `test_agent_types` expected list to include `"investigator"`.
handoff_summary.risks: The test_repo_config_matches_defaults test asserts cfg.agent_types == default.agent_types; both now include investigator in matching order so this passes. No other tests reference the agent_types list directly in a way that would break.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester should run test_config.py (particularly test_agent_types and test_repo_config_matches_defaults) to confirm both pass, plus any other tests that reference AGENT_TYPES (e.g. scheduler validation tests).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Sibling beads that add new agent-type entries or modify AGENT_TYPES/agent_types defaults may conflict with these changes. File-scope overlap on models.py, config.py, and test_config.py is the main risk.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: investigator is added to AGENT_TYPES (the runtime validation set) and agent_types config list, but deliberately excluded from MUTATING_AGENTS. This is consistent with how review and planner are treated — they inspect/read code but do not write or modify it. The ordering in the agent_types list places investigator last (after recovery) to minimise diff noise and preserve existing ordering conventions.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) test_agent_types passes with the updated expected list; (2) test_repo_config_matches_defaults passes confirming .takt/config.yaml stays in parity with default_config(); (3) Any scheduler or validation code that gates on AGENT_TYPES still accepts investigator; (4) MUTATING_AGENTS does NOT include investigator — confirm any conflict-check or followup-creation code that reads MUTATING_AGENTS still behaves correctly.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The bundled _data/default_config.yaml previously did not include `recovery` (pre-existing inconsistency with default_config()), so adding `investigator` there does not fix that gap. That inconsistency is out of scope for this bead. The skills.py allowlist for `investigator` is handled by a separate bead.

conflict_risks: Sibling beads that add new agent-type entries or modify AGENT_TYPES/agent_types defaults may conflict with these changes. File-scope overlap on models.py, config.py, and test_config.py is the main risk.

execution_history.summary: Added `investigator` to AGENT_TYPES in models.py and to the agent_types list in config.py (both OrchestratorConfig field default and default_config()), the bundled default_config.yaml, the repo .takt/config.yaml, and updated the test_agent_types assertion to match. investigator is excluded from MUTATING_AGENTS throughout.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
